data_gap_candidates: constructed welfare ledger by episode and category
OECD pharmaceutical spending and price indices
city rent microdata by controlled versus uncontrolled status
constructed rent-gap plus quantity, queue, quality, and stock-flow ledger
AirDNA or municipal short-term rental registries
US Census Building Permits Survey metro data
constructed welfare ledger from rent gaps, quantities, wait times, quality, and supply response
controlled vs parallel quantity and price panels
subsidy incidence by income or firm age
capped vs uncapped category scanner data
monthly category shortage indicators beyond petroleum and beef

Binding rent control initiates a three-order causal chain

Market-process political economy predicts this claim should hold: Countries maintaining long-lived food price controls or state procurement show slower agricultural value-added growth than market-priced peers.

sustained household fuel or electricity price controls predict higher shortage frequency, larger fiscal subsidy burdens, and lower energy-sector investment.
